name: torrent-match
short_description: Media identification system for torrent names using multi-parser consensus and intelligent LLM fallback.
long_description: Torrent Match is a robust Python library for identifying media content (movies and TV shows) from torrent names and file structures. It uses a sophisticated multi-parser consensus system that runs GuessIt, PTN, ReBulk, and Regex parsers simultaneously, combining their results through weighted voting to achieve accurate detection. When consensus confidence is low, it automatically invokes an LLM parser as a fallback for difficult or ambiguous titles. The system distinguishes between granular media types (movies, TV episodes, season packs, multi-season packs) and integrates with TMDB for validation and enrichment with IMDB IDs, metadata, genres, and ratings. It supports batch processing with parallel execution, direct torrent file parsing, and includes a complete dataset processing pipeline for analyzing large torrent collections.

modules.description: Public API package exports providing clean interface for match, match_batch, match_torrent_file functions and TorrentMatcher class.
Core matching API with simplified functions and TorrentMatcher class for torrent content identification and batch processing.
Command-line interface providing identify, batch, process-dataset, analyze, and test commands for torrent analysis.
Parses and decodes .torrent files to extract names, file structures, and metadata.
Main TorrentContentDetector orchestrator coordinating multi-parser consensus, TMDB validation, and confidence scoring.
Data models for MediaType, ConfidenceLevel, ParseResult, and MediaIdentification objects.
Base Parser abstract class defining standard interface for all parser implementations.
GuessIt parser implementation (weight 1.0) - most reliable for extracting titles and media metadata.
PTN parser implementation (weight 0.8) - secondary parser for title and media type extraction.
ReBulk parser implementation (weight 0.6) - pattern-based parser for content detection.
Regex parser implementation (weight 0.2) - basic fallback using regular expression patterns.
LLM parser using OpenAI/OpenRouter API as intelligent fallback for ambiguous torrent names.
TMDB integration for validating parsing results and retrieving IMDB IDs, titles, and basic metadata.
TMDB enricher providing comprehensive media information including cast, crew, genres, ratings, and poster downloads.
Extracts specific episode and season information from file structures and filenames.

author: Philip Orange <git@philiporange.com>
features: Multi-parser consensus with weighted voting system
Automatic LLM fallback for low-confidence results
Parser selection to use specific parsers only
Granular media type classification (movie, tv_episode, tv_season, tv_multi_season)
TMDB integration for validation and metadata enrichment
Dual output modes (simple and detailed metadata)
Dataset processing pipeline with analysis tools
TMDB enricher with offline SQLite cache capability
